headline: Micron is like the 'bottleneck within the bottleneck' of memory trade
summary: Memory chipmaker Micron Technology (MU) may have shed its post-earnings stock gains, but its shares are still up nearly 250% year-to-date in 2026. Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi is joined by Senior Markets Reporter Ines Ferré and EMJ Capital founder and president Eric Jackson to discuss Micron's performance and the momentum behind the memory chip trade.

headline: Microsoft stock target cut at Wolfe on higher capex estimates
summary: Investing.com -- Wolfe Research revealed in a note on Monday that it has cut its price target on Microsoft to $525 from $570, pointing to surging memory prices that have forced the firm to raise its fiscal 2027 capital expenditure estimate to $270 billion from $230 billion, while maintaining its Outperform rating.

headline: Micron, Ford sign semiconductor supply agreement for vehicles
summary: Micron Technology and Ford Motor on Monday signed a long-term agreement to ‌secure the supply for memory and storage ‌platforms used in the automaker's next-generation vehicle production. This comes days after ​Micron's similar agreement with General Motors, as part of the chipmaker's ongoing investments to expand manufacturing in the United States for automotive customers, including its expansion ‌of advanced DRAM production ⁠in Virginia. DRAM, or dynamic random-access memory, is a critical component for servers ⁠that power cloud computing, databases and AI workloads.

content.title: Micron, Ford sign semiconductor supply agreement for vehicles
content.summary: Micron Technology and Ford Motor on Monday signed a long-term agreement to ‌secure the supply for memory and storage ‌platforms used in the automaker's next-generation vehicle production. This comes days after ​Micron's similar agreement with General Motors, as part of the chipmaker's ongoing investments to expand manufacturing in the United States for automotive customers, including its expansion ‌of advanced DRAM production ⁠in Virginia. DRAM, or dynamic random-access memory, is a critical component for servers ⁠that power cloud computing, databases and AI workloads.

headline: BOW RIVER CAPITAL SUCCESSFULLY COMPLETES DISPOSITION FROM REAL ESTATE FUND II WITH SALE OF FULLER84 BUSINESS PARK
summary: Bow River Capital, a Denver-based private alternative asset firm, today announced that its Real Estate strategy sold Fuller84 Business Park, a 439,600-square-foot, three-building Class A industrial portfolio located in Nampa, Idaho, within the Boise metropolitan area. The transaction closed on June 30, 2026, and represents a successful disposition from Bow River's Real Estate Fund II.

headline: This Week In AI Chips - AI Surge Fuels MLCC Demand and Supply Challenges
summary: Recent developments in the AI chip sector have led to a significant surge in demand for high-end multilayer ceramic capacitors (MLCCs), crucial components in AI server platform upgrades and custom ASIC development by cloud service providers. According to TrendForce, the book-to-bill ratios for leading Japanese and Korean MLCC suppliers have reached their highest levels since the pandemic, suggesting potential supply shortages. The AI-driven demand has caused a shift in production priorities...

According to TrendForce, the book-to-bill ratios for leading Japanese and Korean MLCC suppliers have reached their highest levels since the pandemic, suggesting potential supply shortages. The AI-driven demand has caused a shift in production priorities...

headline: Rebounds for AI stocks help support Wall Street and keep the market mixed
summary: The strength for companies in the artificial-intelligence technology industry had the Nasdaq composite 1.1% higher, as of 9:35 a.m. Eastern time, but the Dow Jones Industrial Average was down 160 points, or 0.3%.  The global appetite for AI from investors will face an additional test later this week when SK Hynix, the South Korean maker of computer memory, plans to raise $28 billion by selling shares of stock that will trade in the United States on the Nasdaq.  SK Hynix’s stock in Seoul has already more than tripled so far this year because of the AI boom, but its day-to-day swings have included sharp losses in recent weeks.

content.title: Rebounds for AI stocks help support Wall Street and keep the market mixed
content.summary: The strength for companies in the artificial-intelligence technology industry had the Nasdaq composite 1.1% higher, as of 9:35 a.m. Eastern time, but the Dow Jones Industrial Average was down 160 points, or 0.3%.  The global appetite for AI from investors will face an additional test later this week when SK Hynix, the South Korean maker of computer memory, plans to raise $28 billion by selling shares of stock that will trade in the United States on the Nasdaq.  SK Hynix’s stock in Seoul has already more than tripled so far this year because of the AI boom, but its day-to-day swings have included sharp losses in recent weeks.

headline: Why Micron’s Stock Dip Is a Buying Opportunity
summary: Micron Technology  and its memory-chip peers have suffered a small dent in the past week, after racking up huge gains in recent months.  Micron shares fell 14% in the past five trading sessions, although the stock was up 1.4% to $988.85 Monday.  Micron has become a volatile stock, with a more than 700% gain in the past 12 months.

headline: Apple's Rally: Pricing Power, AI Discipline, And The Memory Crunch
summary: Apple (AAPL) stock dropped 6% on June 25 after the company raised Mac and iPad prices by $100 to $300, blaming a memory shortage CEO Tim Cook called a "hundred-year flood." Less than two weeks later, shares have clawed back most of that loss, trading near 52-week highs. The market appears to be recalculating Apple's position on two fronts: how well placed Apple is inside the DRAM shortage relative to its peers and how its restraint on AI spending looks increasingly like a smart move.

content.title: Apple's Rally: Pricing Power, AI Discipline, And The Memory Crunch
content.summary: Apple (AAPL) stock dropped 6% on June 25 after the company raised Mac and iPad prices by $100 to $300, blaming a memory shortage CEO Tim Cook called a "hundred-year flood." Less than two weeks later, shares have clawed back most of that loss, trading near 52-week highs. The market appears to be recalculating Apple's position on two fronts: how well placed Apple is inside the DRAM shortage relative to its peers and how its restraint on AI spending looks increasingly like a smart move.

headline: Wait! Michael Burry Is Right About the Memory Boom Ending? This Analyst Says Yes
summary: The AI infrastructure boom has rewritten the rules of the semiconductor industry. Over the past 18 months, memory chips transformed from commodity products into one of the world’s most valuable bottlenecks. High-bandwidth memory (HBM), DRAM, and enterprise NAND became indispensable for Nvidia‘s (NASDAQ:NVDA) AI accelerators, sending memory manufacturers into one of their strongest upcycles in ... Wait! headline: How SK Hynix Can Save the AI Rally—or Send Tech Stocks Tumbling Again
summary: SK Hynix’s planned U.S. listing this week will test the AI-fueled chip rally, offering investors a chance to buy a memory chip maker not named Micron Technology—or to stay away from a group that may have come too far, too fast.  U.S. investors can get exposure to the country with the iShares MSCI Korea exchange-traded fund, but buying  Samsung Electronics  or SK Hynix individually has been almost impossible.

U.S. investors can get exposure to the country with the iShares MSCI Korea exchange-traded fund, but buying  Samsung Electronics  or SK Hynix individually has been almost impossible.
